candidates.name: Andy Beshear
candidates.notes: Two-term Dem governor in deep-red KY (Trump won KY by 30+ pts in 2024). Deacon at Beargrass Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) in Louisville. Grandfather was Baptist minister. Leans heavily into faith messaging — forthcoming book 'Go and Do Likewise' references Good Samaritan parable. VETOED ban on gender-affirming care for minors, calling it 'too much government interference in personal healthcare.' Vetoed conversion therapy ban repeal. Vetoed 5 anti-abortion bills (all overridden by supermajority). Called for red flag law after Louisville bank shooting that killed his mentor Tommy Elliott. NRA 0% rating — 'F' grade. Supports background checks but opposes assault weapons ban. Accepted refugees under federal program. Said ICE is 'going too far' but supports border security in principle. Kentucky Family Foundation criticized his faith as inconsistent with biblical teaching.

profile.education: Vanderbilt University (BA, Political Science & Anthropology); University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and son of former Governor Steve Beshear; served as Kentucky Attorney General (2016-2019); became 63rd governor in 2019.

candidates.name: Mitch McConnell
candidates.notes: Longest-serving Senate leader in US history. Senate Republican Leader since 2007. Retiring at end of current term (2026). Heritage Action LIFETIME 59% — dragged down by 118th Congress score of just 37%. Was 64% (116th) and 61% (117th). Establishment Republican who often prioritized institutional Senate norms over conservative movement priorities. Confirmed 3 Trump SCOTUS justices (Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, Barrett) — blocked Merrick Garland. Voted to acquit Trump in both impeachments but publicly condemned Jan 6 actions. Supported Ukraine aid over objections from MAGA wing. NRA A+ rating, received $1.2M+ in NRA contributions over career. Southern Baptist (converted from Presbyterian). Froze publicly at press conferences in 2023, raising health concerns. Stepped down from leadership in 2025.

profile.education: University of Louisville (BA Political Science), University of Kentucky College of Law (JD)
profile.background: Longest-serving Senate party leader in history. Former Senate Majority and Minority Leader. Former Jefferson County Judge-Executive. In Senate since 1985. Childhood polio survivor.

district_offices.address: 241 E. Main St., Room 102, Federal Building

candidates.name: Rand Paul
candidates.notes: Three-term senator. Heritage Action LIFETIME 90% — one of highest in Senate. 94% in 117th Congress, 91% in 115th. Libertarian-conservative — 'constitutional conservative.' Son of Ron Paul. Eye surgeon (ophthalmologist). Proposed abolishing all foreign aid. Led massive federal spending cuts including defense. Filibustered Brennan CIA nomination (13 hours on drones). Supports medical marijuana legalization as states' rights issue. Critic of 'war on drugs.' Does NOT support same-sex marriage ban at federal level (libertarian position — leave to states). NRA 'A' rating. Baptized Episcopalian, attended Baylor (Baptist), now attends Presbyterian church (has also attended United Methodist). Said 'my faith has never been easy for me' — more private about religion than most GOP senators. Attacked by neighbor in 2017 (broken ribs). Opposes government surveillance. ACLU ally on civil liberties.

profile.education: Baylor University (attended), Duke University School of Medicine (MD)
profile.background: Ophthalmologist and libertarian-leaning Republican. Son of former Rep. Ron Paul. Established free eye surgery program for underserved. In Senate since 2011. 2016 presidential candidate.
